headline: Europe's Air Pollution Drops Sharply Over a Decade
meta_description: Industrial sulphur pollution and nitrogen oxides have fallen dramatically across Europe since 2015, marking a major victory for cleaner transport and industry.
summary_short: Europe's air is getting cleaner. Sulphur pollution is down 59% and nitrogen oxides down 39% since 2015, thanks to cleaner industry and transport.
body:
Across Europe, the air quality has improved measurably over the past decade. Since 2015, industrial sulphur pollution has fallen by 59 percent, while nitrogen oxides—a major contributor to smog and respiratory problems—have dropped by 39 percent. These gains reflect sustained efforts to tighten emissions standards for factories and vehicles across the continent.

The improvements show that policy and innovation can work together to create real change. Cleaner manufacturing processes, stricter vehicle emission standards, and the gradual shift toward renewable energy have all played a role in reducing harmful pollutants that harm human health and the environment. As more industries adopt cleaner technologies and more people switch to electric transport, the trend is expected to continue.

For the millions of people living in Europe's cities and industrial regions, these changes mean cleaner air to breathe and a lower risk of air-pollution-related illness. The data proves that large-scale environmental improvement is possible when nations commit to sustained action.
